prompt: The method should print all permutations but prints duplicates. What is the bug?
choices: The base case should check start == arr.length - 1
The missing backtracking swap after the recursive call leaves the array in a modified state, causing subsequent iterations to generate permutations from wrong prefixes
swap(arr, start, i) should be swap(arr, i, start) with reversed arguments
Permutations require a separate visited boolean array
explanation: After permute(arr, start+1) returns, the array has been modified by the swap. Without swapping back (swap(arr, start, i) again), subsequent iterations of the loop use the corrupted array state, producing wrong permutations. Fix: add swap(arr, start, i); after the recursive call.
